items.title: Coroner finds baby's death after home birth was preventable

children.content: A Victorian coroner has found a baby's death could have been avoided if his mother had been transferred to hospital hours earlier, after signs of an obstructed labour.

gallery.altText: A picture of an emergency sign, with carpark, ambulances and windows in background, outside a hospital's Emergency Department

thumbnail.altText: A picture of an emergency sign, with carpark, ambulances and windows in background, outside a hospital's Emergency Department

items.title: Erin Patterson mushroom trial appeal set after 'miscarriage of justice' claim

children.content: Erin Patterson's appeal against her triple murder conviction will be heard in August, Victoria's Supreme Court has confirmed, with the director of public prosecutions also challenging the length of her jail term.

image.altText: Erin Patterson being led into a prison van

items.title: Violent teen on probation before causing fatal Melbourne crash

children.content: A Melbourne teenager who had been on probation for just nine days when he caused a deadly, high-speed crash is also facing court over a bashing that left a man with life-long disabilities.

image.altText: A badly damaged car crashed into a pole.

items.title: Surging Blues challenging Cats after Daniher tribute at the MCG

children.content: Neale Daniher is remembered with an impeccably observed minute's silence at headquarters, as Carlton hunts a third win in a row under interim boss Josh Fraser. Follow live.

image.altText: Geelong run through a banner that reads RIP Neale Daniher

items.title: Victorian MP sorry for mistakenly saying alleged koala killer had died

children.content: State MP Jacinta Ermacora says "they were misinformed and passed that misinformation on".

image.altText: A koala takes a nap in a tree

items.title: John Farnham says he won't ever sing on stage again ahead of tribute concert

children.content: Jimmy Barnes, Tina Arena and Human Nature will take the stage to honour Australian legend John Farnham at a tribute concert later this year.

items.title: Firefighters look to restart PFAS case against manufacturer 3M

children.content: The federal government's $2 billion lawsuit against "forever chemicals" manufacturer 3M has Fire Rescue Victoria considering whether it should relaunch its own legal proceedings.

image.altText: Firefighters spray foam on a structure in a national park

items.title: Women's shed ex-board member banned over alleged transphobic comments

children.content: A former board member of a women's shed in Victoria has been evicted after allegedly "spewing" discriminatory and hurtful comments about transgender people.

image.altText: a close up of a shed and a sign that says 'Women's Shed Ballarat'

items.title: Former Victorian premier's new fence angers neighbours

children.content: A regional Victorian council says it is investigating complaints about 500 metres of fencing that was recently built along former premier John Brumby's property.

image.altText: Man in vest stands in front of intersection.

items.title: People trapped in new housing estate traffic bottlenecks welcome funding

children.content: Motorists in Melbourne's outer north welcome funds for "lifeline" outer suburbs road, but say more needs to be done.

image.altText: Cars queue along road in peak hour traffic with a form one lane sign in the foreground

items.title: Kids protest against McDonald's after fast-food giant buys land in small town

children.content: Children turn out in protest after it's revealed the global fast-food giant has paid $4.6 million for a prominent site in central Victoria's Castlemaine.

gallery.altText: A group of children at a protest

thumbnail.altText: A group of children at a protest

items.title: Officeworks to offshore hundreds of jobs in search for white-collar workers

children.content: The Australian office products retailer is planning to offshore hundreds of jobs to India and the Philippines, joining a growing trend of businesses hoping to find more highly skilled white-collar workers overseas.

image.altText: A large outdoor Officeworks sign in front of large trees.

items.title: Victoria's political donations cap to double to $10,000 under proposed changes

children.content: The cap on political donations in Victoria will be at least doubled and public funding for political parties increased under government proposals to rewrite the regime struck out by the High Court.

image.altText: Looking up at the pillars outside the Victorian Parliament.

items.title: Man arrested over alleged 1994 armed robbery at Melbourne shopping centre

children.content: A man who allegedly carried out an armed robbery and shot three men at Chadstone Shopping Centre in 1994 has been arrested by police in Queensland.

image.altText: The exterior of Chadstone Shopping Centre with a Christmas wreath hanging as decoration on it.

items.title: Lawyer says Joshua Brown will admit to childcare centre sex crimes

children.content: Joshua Brown, who has been charged with 156 offences, including sexual assault, has appeared in the Melbourne Magistrates' Court via video link.

image.altText: A man smiles in a photo with coworkers

items.title: From Torquay to Antarctica, wagtail paintings are popping up everywhere

children.content: A Victorian artist paints hundreds of willie wagtails a week to give away locally and now they're popping up all over the world.

gallery.altText: Mike McLean paints his willie wagtails on pieces of reclaimed timber.
Harper, on her bike, sees a red wagtail painting next to a letterbox.
Two kids point to a green wagtail painting on a fence.

thumbnail.altText: Mike McLean paints his willie wagtails on pieces of reclaimed timber.
Harper, on her bike, sees a red wagtail painting next to a letterbox.
Two kids point to a green wagtail painting on a fence.

items.title: Teen accused of home invasion granted bail by Court of Appeal

children.content: After three months on remand, a teen who was refused bail on a Mildura home invasion charge has been released.

image.altText: A roundabout, with a red of red flowers in front of a brci building surround by palm trees

items.title: Woman charged with joining IS remanded in custody

children.content: Police conducted searches at homes in Melbourne's north early on Thursday as part of ongoing investigations into people who have returned from Syria.

image.altText: A woman in black Islamic garb is flanked by two uniformed police officers.

items.title: 'Remarkable' large funnel cloud spotted in regional Victoria

children.content: The Bureau of Meteorology says it is unusual to see large funnel clouds so far west in Victoria.

image.altText: A photo of a large waterspout pouring from the sky

items.title: Fears for teacher who last contacted family a week ago after trip to India

children.content: The family of maths teacher Sunil Sharma say the 66-year-old disappeared nearly a week ago, while trying to sell a property in the Punjabi city of Amritsar.

image.altText: Surbhi and Sunil Sharma smile, taking a selfie together in a city.

items.title: PM response to truth-telling process 'hugely significant'

children.content: The Walk for Truth ended at Parliament House today and while there was not a firm commitment from the PM, many felt the response was a 'positive move'.

gallery.altText: Anthony albanese shaking Travis lovett hands

thumbnail.altText: Anthony albanese shaking Travis lovett hands

items.title: Coroner finds smuggled drugs cause of inmate's death

children.content: A 35-year-old inmate at Victoria's Fulham Correctional Centre died of a drug overdose after consuming drugs smuggled into the jail, a coronial investigation has found.

image.altText: An aerial shot of a correctional centre

items.title: Teenager stabbed at Melbourne shopping centre as police release knife data

children.content: Victoria Police reveal they seized an average of 48 knives, machetes and "zombie knives" daily so far in 2026.

image.altText: Two police cars with blue and white checks parked outside a grey building with a sign that says "Hoyts" at night.

items.title: Explosions heard as blaze destroys Brunswick bike shop

children.content: Firefighters responded to separate blazes in Melbourne overnight, with police investigating one as an arson attack.

image.altText: A firefighter stands at the top of a high ladder aiming water at the roof of a building where flames can be seen at night.

items.title: Load limits on Victorian bridges 'wreaking havoc' on agricultural industry

children.content: Councils across regional Victoria say they are being forced to ban trucks from some bridges because they cannot afford to fix them to keep them safe.

image.altText: A road on a small bridge.

items.title: From 'the Reverend' to the 'Big Freeze', Neale Daniher never stopped leading

children.content: Neale Daniher showed leadership on and off the field throughout his life, from club captaincy to coaching and as Australian of the Year — but no more so when facing the challenge of an MND diagnosis.

articles.title: Neale Daniher to be remembered with state funeral, premier reveals

children.content: A great of the game and icon after his retirement, Neale Daniher is being remembered as a fighter and an inspiration after his death.

image.altText: Neale Daniher sits in a wheelchair front of Melbourne Demons fans.

articles.title: Neale Daniher, former AFL player and motor neurone disease campaigner, dies

children.content: Former Essendon player and Demons coach Neale Daniher has died, aged 65. He was diagnosed with motor neurone disease in 2013 and campaigned to raise awareness of the disease and increase research.

image.altText: An older man wears a blue beanie with a logo saying "Big Freeze 2: Cure for MND Foundation".

gallery.altText: Former AFL player and coach Neale Daniher stands smiling on a stage in front of hundreds of Melbourne fans.

thumbnail.altText: Former AFL player and coach Neale Daniher stands smiling on a stage in front of hundreds of Melbourne fans.

items.title: Copi Mineral Sands Project near Victoria-NSW border gets green light

children.content: A mineral sands project of global strategic importance receives provisional approval from the New South Wales government.

image.altText: Farmland in the Australian outback, with a blue sky, red dirt and dried grass and scrub

items.title: Young farmer who died two weeks after engagement set to leave lasting legacy

children.content: A scholarship will be created at a Victorian agricultural college in memory of a young man who died in a farming accident last month.

image.altText: Man and woman wearing cowboy hats, sitting on a log

items.title: Court increases jail sentence for remorseless sex worker killer

children.content: Xiaozheng Lin will spend more time in prison for killing two sex workers in 2022, following an appeal by the prosecutor.

image.altText: Xiaozheng Lin stands against a grey backdrop in a police mug shot.

items.title: Teaching student charged with online child abuse offences

children.content: A Victorian teaching student, who had recently worked as a childcare centre cook, a foster carer
and was a school council president, is facing charges relating to online child abuse material.

image.altText: A man with glasses smiles at the camera.

items.title: ISIS-linked woman barred from Australia tried to board flight

children.content: An Australian woman with links to ISIS, who is barred from Australia on a temporary exclusion order, tried to board a flight home with the other members of Tuesday's returning cohort, a Senate estimates hearing has been told.

image.altText: A Qatar plane on the tarmac at Melbourne international airport.

items.title: Two men released without charge after Dezi Freeman-related arrest

children.content: A 48-year-old and a 45-year-old were taken into custody by detectives from Taskforce Summit, who continued to investigate Freeman's movements after the fatal shooting of officers Neal Thompson and Vadim de Waart-Hottart at a Porepunkah property in Victoria's north-east in August last year.

image.altText: Dezi Freeman pictured outside a court in Wangaratta.

items.title: Scott reveals he wanted to trade Merrett, confirms bumper Reid offer

children.content: The ousted Essendon coach reveals he was against the decision to keep Zach Merrett at the club despite a late trade offer from Hawthorn.

image.altText: Brad Scott and Zach Merrett stand together on Anzac Day

items.title: Reception hours slashed at police stations in Melbourne's outer-north

children.content: Victoria Police says its decision to cut the hours police stations are open to the public in Melbourne's outer north will result in more police on the frontline.

image.altText: A police badge on an arm.
